Title: A Sugarcane G-Protein-Coupled Receptor, ShGPCR1, Confers Tolerance to Multiple Abiotic Stresses

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Multiple ShGPCR1 alleles were cloned from sugarcane and energy cane; alleles show ~98% nucleotide identity and largely conserved coding sequence, indicating strong purifying selection. The predominant full-length allele is 1,407 bp (468 aa), and the EC2 allele was chosen for overexpression in sugarcane. ShGPCR1 is highly similar (∼96–99%) to GPCR-like proteins from rice COLD1, sorghum, and maize, clustering phylogenetically with monocot GPCRs.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ShGPCR1 encodes a conserved GPCR-like protein with nine transmembrane helices and characteristic regulatory motifs, closely related to rice COLD1 and other monocot GPCRs.
2. ShGPCR1 transcripts are significantly induced by drought, salinity, cold, and ABA in sugarcane leaves and culms.
3. ShGPCR1 is predominantly localized at the plasma membrane in sugarcane embryogenic cells.
4. Constitutive overexpression of ShGPCR1 in sugarcane confers enhanced tolerance to drought, high salinity, and cold without detectable growth penalties under non-stress conditions.
5. ShGPCR1-OE plants maintain higher relative water content, root biomass, and overall biomass under drought and exhibit reduced foliar damage and higher survival under salinity and cold.
6. Overexpression of ShGPCR1 is associated with increased expression of multiple drought-, salinity-, and cold-responsive marker genes and the antioxidant CAT gene under stress.
7. ShGPCR1 overexpression significantly enhances GTP-induced cytosolic Ca2+ elevation, while global ionomycin-induced Ca2+ levels remain unchanged, consistent with a role in GPCR-mediated Ca2+ signaling during abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative analysis using sugarcane expressed sequence tag assemblies from the SUCEST database and publicly available sugarcane RNA-seq datasets from NCBI SRA to assemble the ShGPCR1 consensus CDS.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression of ShGPCR1 in sugarcane confers enhanced tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses (drought, salinity, cold), with delayed and reduced leaf wilting, chlorosis, and necrosis, improved survival, and maintenance of root growth, relative water content, and biomass, without detectable negative effects on growth under non-stress conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Plasma membrane-localized GPCR-like receptor that, when overexpressed, enhances GTP-dependent increases in cytosolic Ca2+ without altering ionomycin-induced global Ca2+ levels, consistent with Ca2+ influx from the apoplast. ShGPCR1 carries multiple predicted N-myristoylation and protein kinase C/casein kinase II phosphorylation sites that are likely important for membrane anchoring and regulation. Overexpression is associated with elevated expression of the antioxidant gene CAT, implying enhanced ROS detoxification under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in a GPCR/G-protein-mediated Ca2+-signaling pathway involved in abiotic stress responses, where plasma membrane-localized ShGPCR1 enhances GTP-triggered Ca2+ influx and is associated with activation of multiple drought-, salinity-, and cold-responsive genes (including LEA, DYH, SCDR4, GOLS, ERF3, SOS1, ShNHX1, SsNAC23, CBF2, ScADH3, and CAT), contributing to adaptive responses to water deficit, high salinity, and low temperature.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Overexpression of ShGPCR1 in sugarcane correlates with and is described as causing elevated expression of multiple stress-responsive genes under the corresponding stresses: ShGPCR1 -->(activation)--> LEA, DYH, SCDR4, and GOLS under drought; ShGPCR1 -->(activation)--> ERF3, SOS1, and ShNHX1 under salinity; ShGPCR1 -->(activation)--> SsNAC23, CBF2, and ScADH3 under cold; ShGPCR1 -->(activation)--> CAT under drought, salinity, and cold, consistent with enhanced ROS-scavenging capacity.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GPCR-like membrane protein with nine predicted transmembrane helices, conserved Ras GTPase-activating protein domain, ATP-/GTP-binding region, multiple N-myristoylation sites, and predicted protein kinase C and casein kinase II phosphorylation sites characteristic of plant GPCRs.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predominantly localized to the plasma membrane, as shown by ShGPCR1::mGFP fluorescence co-localizing with the plasma membrane dye FM4-64 and not with DAPI-stained nuclei in transiently transformed sugarcane embryogenic leaf rolls.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: ShGPCR1 transcript levels are significantly induced by abiotic stresses and ABA: under drought, 5.2-fold in culms and 2.4-fold in leaves; under salinity, 7.1-fold in culms and 2.2-fold in leaves; under cold, modest but significant induction (~1.2–1.37-fold) in leaves and culms; ShGPCR1 is also induced by exogenous ABA treatment.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Sh_221E20_t000010 (matching transcript in the draft sugarcane genome)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by withholding water for 40 days until soil moisture reached ~10–15%; salinity stress by weekly soil drenching with 200 mM NaCl for 2 weeks; cold/chilling stress by exposure to 4°C for up to 3 weeks followed by −5°C or −20°C for 4 h; ABA treatments (10–100 nM) on detached leaves; GTP and ionomycin treatments during Ca2+ imaging.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Engineering abiotic stress tolerance (drought, salinity, and cold) in sugarcane via a G-protein-coupled receptor-mediated signaling pathway.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress: drought tolerance, salinity (salt) tolerance, and cold (chilling/freezing) tolerance; drought-associated growth and biomass traits.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought, ShGPCR1 expression increases 5.2-fold in culms and 2.4-fold in leaves; under salinity, 7.1-fold in culms and 2.2-fold in leaves; under cold, ~1.2–1.37-fold in leaves and culms. Drought marker genes LEA, DYH, SCDR4, and GOLS are induced by approximately 7.7–15.1-fold, 3.4–6.2-fold, 2.4–6.2-fold, and 2.2–6.0-fold, respectively, in ShGPCR1-OE lines versus NT under drought. After 40 days of drought, NT leaf relative water content drops to 51.8% of pre-drought levels, whereas ShGPCR1-OE lines retain ~73.7–79.2%. Salt marker genes ERF3, SOS1, and ShNHX1 are upregulated ~1.2–3.5-fold in ShGPCR1-OE lines versus NT under salinity, and cold marker genes SsNAC23, CBF2, and ScADH3 are induced ~1.4–15.4-fold under cold. GTP-induced Ca2+ release (Fluo-4 fluorescence) is significantly higher in ShGPCR1-OE than NT cells (0.048 ± 0.002 vs 0.013 ± 0.002; p = 2.5E−22), while ionomycin-induced global Ca2+ levels do not differ significantly.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under prolonged drought, ShGPCR1-OE lines exhibit better-developed root systems and significantly higher dry root weight than NT plants, as well as improved culm height, culm diameter, leaf length, and total biomass; under non-stress conditions, ShGPCR1-OE and NT plants show similar growth, indicating no obvious yield or growth penalty. Overexpression of ShGPCR1 is associated with increased expression of the CAT antioxidant gene under drought, salinity, and cold, suggesting enhanced ROS-scavenging capacity.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Embryogenic leaf rolls and calli from sugarcane (Saccharum spp. hybrids) variety CP72-1210; ShGPCR1-overexpressing (ShGPCR1-OE) transgenic lines 1–3; non-transgenic (NT) sugarcane controls; greenhouse- and growth-chamber-grown seedlings and potted plants.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Sugarcane variety CP72-1210 (transformation background); energy cane variety TCP10-4928 used for allele cloning
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Comparative genomics and BLAST searches against SUCEST and NCBI SRA to identify ShGPCR1; RT-PCR cloning and Sanger sequencing of alleles from sugarcane and energy cane; in silico motif and transmembrane prediction; Southern blot to assess transgene integration; RT-qPCR and RT-PCR to quantify ShGPCR1 and stress marker gene expression; biolistic transformation to generate ShGPCR1-OE sugarcane lines; drought, salinity, and cold stress assays in greenhouse and growth chambers; measurement of relative water content, agronomic traits, and biomass; transient expression of ShGPCR1::mGFP in embryogenic leaf rolls and confocal imaging with FM4-64 and DAPI; Ca2+ imaging in leaf cells using Fluo-4 AM with GTP and ionomycin treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by constitutive overexpression of a full-length ShGPCR1 CDS (EC2 allele) in transgenic sugarcane (CP72-1210 background) followed by: (1) molecular confirmation of transgene integration (PCR, Southern blot) and overexpression (RT-PCR/RT-qPCR); (2) phenotypic assays under controlled drought, salinity, and cold stress regimes in greenhouse/growth chambers; (3) measurement of stress marker gene expression by RT-PCR/RT-qPCR; (4) subcellular localization of ShGPCR1::mGFP by transient expression and confocal microscopy; and (5) cellular Ca2+ imaging with Fluo-4 AM to test GTP-induced Ca2+ responses.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified by comparative genomics using a rice GPCR ortholog (LOC_Os04g51180.1) as query in BLAST searches against the SUCEST EST database and sugarcane RNA-seq reads (NCBI SRA), followed by in silico assembly of a 1,407-bp consensus CDS (ShGPCR1) and confirmation by mapping to a single matching transcript in the draft sugarcane genome; endogenous CDS amplified from sugarcane and energy cane by RT-PCR and cloned for sequencing and overexpression.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of ShGPCR1 in sugarcane produces lines with significantly enhanced tolerance to drought, salinity, and cold stresses while maintaining normal growth and biomass under non-stress conditions. ShGPCR1-OE plants retain higher relative water content, root biomass, and total biomass during prolonged water deficit, and show reduced foliar damage and better survival under high salinity and chilling/freezing treatments. These traits indicate that ShGPCR1 is a promising target for engineering or breeding sugarcane cultivars adapted to marginal, water- and salt-limited environments, potentially improving yield stability and biomass production without evident growth penalties.
